mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 20 days, or the interaction term coefficient is insignificant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, providing a consistent upward drift to S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested regimes, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return distribution, rejecting the positive earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A break below the six-month moving average in industrial production signals a sudden supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average.
